resources.string: Hooks the system framework to auto-detect and silence notification channels matching keywords like "marketing". Supports regex and batch regex rules, with built-in safe mode and hook logging.
Root not granted — the module may not work properly
Fine-tune per-app notification channels with regex and per-channel overrides.

Safe mode is a protection that trips automatically when System UI keeps crashing. Once you\'ve confirmed your block rules are fixed, clearing the flag restores blocking.
The module watches this flag from inside the system process, so it usually recovers in place without a reboot. If it does not recover after a while, reboot once manually.
Cleared. Blocking will resume automatically, no reboot needed.
Also checks the channel description for a more complete match.

Enter one regex rule per line (use | within a line to match multiple keywords)
Examples: .*营销.* .*(推广|促销|优惠).* ^ads?_.*

Matches a notification\'s title/text and blocks the whole notification — useful for marketing pushed on a shared or "default" channel that channel-level blocking cannot reach. Applies to every notification; after updating the module, reboot once to load the hook, then switches and rule edits take effect instantly. Uses its own rules below, and is protected by the same allow-whitelist above; foreground-service notifications are never blocked. Off by default — enable with care.

Matches a notification\'s title/text and blocks the whole notification — useful for marketing pushed on a shared or "default" channel that channel-level blocking cannot reach. Applies to every notification; switches and rule edits take effect instantly, no reboot needed. Uses its own rules below, and is protected by the same allow-whitelist above; foreground-service notifications are never blocked. Off by default — enable with care.
Matches content when a notification is enqueued.

Content-block regex, one rule per line (use | within a line to match multiple keywords)
Example: .*(限时特惠|内购|直播间).*
When off, shows every channel the hook has recorded.
When off, shows every channel recorded so far.
Reset %1$d item(s) to default (follow regex rules)
The selected channels have no override to clear
Multi-select mode: batch buttons apply only to the %1$d checked item(s)
Normal mode: batch buttons apply to the %1$d currently shown item(s)
No data yet. Tap refresh after an app creates a notification channel; if still empty, check root read access.

No data yet. Root-free mode can only record a channel once it has actually posted a notification, so this list fills in as you use the device; channels that existed before you enabled it and have not posted since will not appear.
No channels currently match the regex rules. Turn off "Show only regex-matched channels" to see everything.
⚠ Hook not active — check that the module is enabled in LSPosed with the "Android System Framework" scope ticked, then reboot
⚠ Module updated — reboot for the new version to take effect (system_server is still running the old hook)
No blocks yet. Once blocks happen, they will be ranked here by app.
No content-level blocks for this app yet.\n(Channel-level blocking only mutes a channel; it does not record individual blocked notifications.)
(This notification had no title or text)
Reset the content-block count and per-app ranking to zero? This does not affect your rules or switches.
Saved and synced to the hook
Saved locally, but syncing to the hook failed — check root authorization
Failed to sync to %1$s — check root authorization
Matched channels are never blocked — this takes priority over the block rules.
Example: .*(验证码|动态密码).* .*(微信|QQ|短信).*
Saved locally, but syncing failed — check root authorization
Notifications from listed apps are never blocked (channel-level and content-level).
No apps added yet. Tap "Add App" to pick apps to fully allow.
Pick one — you can switch anytime from Settings.
Requires root and LSPosed, with this module added to the Xposed scope. Blocked channels are fully silent — notifications never appear at all.

Only needs "Notification access" granted in system settings — no root / Magisk / KernelSU / LSPosed. Due to platform limits, a blocked notification briefly appears (possibly with a sound) before being cancelled; pre-existing channels that never trigger again cannot be discovered.
Notification access, battery optimisation and background survival
Lets the app keep running in the background
Xiaomi\'s own power manager, separate from battery optimisation; state unreadable
Lets the app be woken; state unreadable
Pull down this app\'s card in recents; no API, manual only

Root-free interception only works while this app\'s process is alive, and stops silently once the system clears it. These settings reduce the chance of that, but do not guarantee it; open the app to restore interception. Root mode needs none of them.

Root-free mode only intercepts while this app\'s process is alive. Setting the power policy to "No restrictions" and locking the app in recents is recommended — otherwise the system may clear it away and interception silently stops.\n\nYou can change these any time under About → Permissions.

In root-free mode: notifications briefly appear before being cancelled; channels that existed before enabling and never fire again cannot be discovered; stats are recorded separately from root mode. For fully silent blocking, use Root + LSPosed mode.
⚠ Root-free mode inactive — grant notification access
⚠ Root-free mode is not intercepting: listener not connected (common after a module update) — reconnecting…
These options are for troubleshooting only — no need to enable them for daily use.
⚠ Enabling log output writes to a file on every channel event, which may slightly affect performance. Turn it off when you\'re done troubleshooting.
When on, hook events are written to hook.log. Off by default.
When on, no logs are sent to the LSPosed module log
Only logs at or above the selected level are sent to LSPosed
